de.uka.ilkd.key.java.recoderext

Class RMethodBodyStatement

  • java.lang.Object
    • recoder.java.JavaSourceElement
      • recoder.java.JavaProgramElement
        • recoder.java.JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
          • recoder.java.statement.JavaStatement
            • de.uka.ilkd.key.java.recoderext.RMethodBodyStatement
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    KeYRecoderExtension, java.io.Serializable, java.lang.Cloneable, ExpressionContainer, NamedProgramElement, NonTerminalProgramElement, ProgramElement, TypeReferenceContainer, SourceElement, Statement, ModelElement, NamedModelElement

    public class RMethodBodyStatement
extends JavaStatement
implements KeYRecoderExtension, TypeReferenceContainer, ExpressionContainer, NamedProgramElement
    See Also:

        Returns the child at the specified index in this node's "virtual" child array
        Specified by:

        Parameters:
        index - an index into this node's "virtual" child array
        Returns:
        the program element at the given position
        Throws:
        java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is out of bounds

      • makeParentRoleValid

        public void makeParentRoleValid()
        Ensures that each child has "this" as syntactical parent.
        Specified by:
        makeParentRoleValid in interface NonTerminalProgramElement
        Overrides:
        makeParentRoleValid in class JavaNonTerminalProgramElement

      • replaceChild

        public boolean replaceChild(ProgramElement p,
                            ProgramElement q)
        Replace a single child in the current node. The child to replace is matched by identity and hence must be known exactly. The replacement element can be null - in that case, the child is effectively removed. The parent role of the new child is validated, while the parent link of the replaced child is left untouched.
        Specified by:
        replaceChild in interface NonTerminalProgramElement
        Parameters:
        p - the old child.
        q - the new child.
        Returns:
        true if a replacement has occured, false otherwise.
        Throws:
        java.lang.ClassCastException - if the new child cannot take over the role of the old one.
